back: A quantitative measure used to evaluate the quality of user experience when interacting with a product or system. Examples include task completion time, error rates, and satisfaction scores.
System Usability Scale - a 10-item questionnaire used to assess perceived usability. Scores range from 0-100; above 68 is considered above average.
Net Promoter Score - measures customer loyalty by asking how likely users are to recommend the product on a 0-10 scale. Score = %Promoters - %Detractors.
Customer Satisfaction Score - measures immediate satisfaction with a product, feature, or interaction. Usually collected via post-interaction surveys.
Customer Effort Score - measures how easy it is for users to accomplish a task. Lower scores indicate less effort required (7-point scale, 1=Strongly Agree it's easy).
The percentage of users who successfully complete a given task without critical errors. Expressed as: (Successful completions / Total attempts) × 100.
The duration taken by a user to complete a specific task from start to finish. Measured in seconds/minutes; shorter times indicate better usability.
The frequency at which users make errors while interacting with a system. Calculated as: (Number of errors / Total interactions) × 100.
A usability inspection method where evaluators examine the interface against recognized usability principles (Nielsen's 10 heuristics) to identify usability issues.
A method comparing two versions of a webpage/app to determine which performs better. Users are randomly assigned to version A or B, and metrics are compared.
The percentage of visitors who leave a website after viewing only one page, without taking any action or navigating further.
The percentage of users who complete a desired action (purchase, sign-up, download) out of total visitors. Higher rates indicate effective UX.
Metrics that capture user perceptions, attitudes, and subjective opinions about a product through surveys and interviews (e.g., SUS, NPS, CSAT).
Metrics that observe and measure actual user actions and behaviors through analytics, such as click paths, time on task, and navigation patterns.
A usability testing method where participants verbalize their thoughts, feelings, and decisions while performing tasks, providing insight into user mental models.
